resourceapi.AllocationConfigSourceClaim,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tRequests:            config.Requests,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tDeviceConfiguration: config.DeviceConfiguration,\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Determine node selector.\n\t\tnodeSelector, err := alloc.createNodeSelector(internalResult.devices, node.Name)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create NodeSelector for claim %s: %w\", claim.Name, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tallocationResult.NodeSelector = nodeSelector\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\nfunc (a *Allocator) GetStats() Stats {\n\ts := Stats{\n\t\tNumAllocateOneInvocations: a.numAllocateOneInvocations.Load(),\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) validateDeviceRequest(request requestAccessor, parentRequest requestAccessor, requestKey requestIndices, pools []*Pool) (requestData, error) {\n\tclaim := alloc.claimsToAllocate[requestKey.claimIndex]\n\trequestData := requestData{\n\t\trequest:       request,\n\t\tparentRequest: parentRequest,\n\t}\n\tfor i, selector := range request.selectors() {\n\t\tif selector.CEL == nil {\n\t\t\t// Unknown future selector type!\n\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s, selector #%d: CEL expression empty (unsupported selector type?)\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), i)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif !alloc.features.AdminAccess && request.hasAdminAccess() {\n\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: admin access is requested, but the feature is disabled\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name())\n\t}\n\n\t// Should be set. If it isn't, something changed and we should refuse to proceed.\n\tif request.deviceClassName() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: missing device class name (unsupported request type?)\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name())\n\t}\n\tclass, err := alloc.classLister.Get(request.deviceClassName())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: could not retrieve device class %s: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), request.deviceClassName(), err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Start collecting information about the request.\n\t// The class must be set and stored before calling isSelectable.\n\trequestData.class = class\n\n\tswitch request.allocationMode() {\n\tcase resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:\n\t\tnumDevices := request.count()\n\t\tif numDevices > math.MaxInt {\n\t\t\t// Allowed by API validation, but doesn't make sense.\n\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: exact count %d is too large\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), numDevices)\n\t\t}\n\t\trequestData.numDevices = int(numDevices)\n\tcase resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll:\n\t\t// If we have any any request that wants \"all\" devices, we need to\n\t\t// figure out how much \"all\" is. If some pool is incomplete, we stop\n\t\t// here because allocation cannot succeed. Once we do scoring, we should\n\t\t// stop in all cases, not just when \"all\" devices are needed, because\n\t\t// pulling from an incomplete might not pick the best solution and it's\n\t\t// better to wait. This does not matter yet as long the incomplete pool\n\t\t// has some matching device.\n\t\trequestData.allDevices = make([]deviceWithID, 0, resourceapi.AllocationResultsMaxSize)\n\t\tfor _, pool := range pools {\n\t\t\tif pool.IsIncomplete {\n\t\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently being updated\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), pool.PoolID)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif pool.IsInvalid {\n\t\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently invalid\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), pool.PoolID)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, slice := range pool.Slices {\n\t\t\t\tfor deviceIndex := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n\t\t\t\t\tselectable, err := alloc.isSelectable(requestKey, requestData, slice, deviceIndex)\n\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn requestData, err\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif selectable {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdevice := deviceWithID{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tid:     DeviceID{Driver: slice.Spec.Driver, Pool: slice.Spec.Pool.Name, Device: slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex].Name},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDevice: &slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex],\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tslice:  slice,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif alloc.features.ConsumableCapacity {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Next validate whether resource request over capacity\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdevice := slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.CmpRequestOverCapacity(requestData.request, slice, device)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Skip comparing device capacity request\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"device\", device, \"request\", requestData.request.name(), \"err\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif !success {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device capacity not enough\", \"device\", device)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\trequestData.allDevices = append(requestData.allDevices, device)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\trequestData.numDevices = len(requestData.allDevices)\n\t\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Request for 'all' devices\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"request\", request.name(), \"numDevicesPerRequest\", requestData.numDevices)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: unsupported count mode %s\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), request.allocationMode())\n\t}\n\treturn requestData, nil\n}\n\n// errStop is a special error that gets returned by allocateOne if it detects\n// that allocation cannot succeed.\nvar errStop = errors.New(\"stop allocation\")\n\n// errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded is a special error that gets return by\n// allocatedOne when the number of allocated devices exceeds the max number\n// allowed. This is checked by earlier invocations in the recursion and used\n// to do more aggressive backtracking and avoid attempting allocations that\n// we know can not succeed.\nvar errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded = errors.New(\"allocation max size exceeded\")\n\n// allocator is used while an [Allocator.Allocate] is running. Only a single\n// goroutine works with it, so there is no need for locking.\ntype allocator struct {\n\t*Allocator\n\tctx                  context.Context\n\tlogger               klog.Logger\n\tnode                 *v1.Node\n\tclaimsToAllocate     []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim\n\tpools                []*Pool\n\tdeviceMatchesRequest map[matchKey]bool\n\tconstraints          [][]constraint // one list of constraints per claim\n\t// consumedCounters keeps track of the counters consumed by all devices\n\t// that are in the process of being allocated.\n\t// The keys in the map are ResourceSlice names.\n\tconsumedCounters map[string]counterSets\n\trequestData      map[requestIndices]requestData // one entry per request with no subrequests and one entry per subrequest\n\t// allocatingDevices tracks which devices will be newly allocated for a\n\t// particular attempt to find a solution. The map is indexed by device\n\t// and its values represent for which of a pod's claims the device will\n\t// be allocated.\n\t// Claims are identified by their index in claimsToAllocate.\n\tallocatingDevices map[DeviceID]sets.Set[int]\n\t// allocatingCapacity tracks the amount of device capacity that will be newly allocated\n\t// for a particular attempt to find a solution.\n\t// The map is indexed by device ID, and each value represents the accumulated capacity\n\t// requested by all allocations targeting that device.\n\tallocatingCapacity ConsumedCapacityCollection\n\tresult             []internalAllocationResult\n}\n\n// counterSets is a map with the name of counter sets to the counters in\n// the set.\ntype counterSets map[draapi.UniqueString]map[string]resourceapi.Counter\n\n// matchKey identifies a device/request pair.\ntype matchKey struct {\n\tDeviceID\n\trequestIndices\n}\n\n// requestIndices identifies one specific request\n// or subrequest by three properties:\n//\n// - claimIndex: The index of the claim in the requestData map.\n// - requestIndex: The index of the request in the claim.\n// - subRequestIndex: The index of the subrequest in the parent request.\ntype requestIndices struct {\n\tclaimIndex, requestIndex int\n\tsubRequestIndex          int\n}\n\n// deviceIndices identifies one specific required device inside\n// a request or subrequest of a certain claim.\ntype deviceIndices struct {\n\tclaimIndex      int // The index of the claim in the allocator.\n\trequestIndex    int // The index of the request in the claim.\n\tsubRequestIndex int // The index of the subrequest within the request (ignored if subRequest is false).\n\tdeviceIndex     int // The index of a device within a request or subrequest.\n}\n\ntype requestData struct {\n\t// The request or subrequest which needs to be allocated.\n\t// Never nil.\n\trequest requestAccessor\n\t// The parent of a subrequest, nil if not a subrequest.\n\tparentRequest requestAccessor\n\tclass         *resourceapi.DeviceClass\n\tnumDevices    int\n\n\t// selectedSubRequestIndex is set for the entry with requestIndices.subRequestIndex == 0.\n\t// It is the index of the subrequest which got picked during allocation.\n\tselectedSubRequestIndex int\n\n\t// pre-determined set of devices for allocating \"all\" devices\n\tallDevices []deviceWithID\n}\n\ntype deviceWithID struct {\n\t*draapi.Device\n\tid    DeviceID\n\tslice *draapi.ResourceSlice\n}\n\ntype internalAllocationResult struct {\n\tdevices []internalDeviceResult\n}\n\ntype internalDeviceResult struct {\n\t*draapi.Device\n\trequest          string // name of the request (if no subrequests) or the subrequest\n\tparentRequest    string // name of the request which contains the subrequest, empty otherwise\n\tid               DeviceID\n\tshareID          *types.UID\n\tslice            *draapi.ResourceSlice\n\tconsumedCapacity map[resourceapi.QualifiedName]resource.Quantity\n\tadminAccess      *bool\n}\n\nfunc (idr internalDeviceResult) requestName() string {\n\tif idr.parentRequest == \"\" {\n\t\treturn idr.request\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", idr.parentRequest, idr.request)\n}\n\nfunc (idr internalDeviceResult) lookupRequest(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) requestAccessor {\n\trequestName := idr.request\n\tif idr.parentRequest != \"\" {\n\t\trequestName = idr.parentRequest\n\t}\n\tfor i := range claim.Spec.Devices.Requests {\n\t\trequest := &claim.Spec.Devices.Requests[i]\n\t\tif request.Name != requestName {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif idr.parentRequest == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// No need to check sub-requests.\n\t\t\treturn &exactDeviceRequestAccessor{request}\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor j := range request.FirstAvailable {\n\t\t\tsubRequest := &request.FirstAvailable[j]\n\t\t\tif subRequest.Name != idr.request {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn &deviceSubRequestAccessor{subRequest}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\ntype constraint interface {\n\t// add is called whenever a device is about to be allocated. It must\n\t// check whether the device matches the constraint and if yes,\n\t// track that it is allocated.\n\tadd(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID) bool\n\n\t// For every successful add there is exactly one matching removed call\n\t// with the exact same parameters.\n\tremove(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID)\n}\n\n// matchAttributeConstraint compares an attribute value across devices.\n// All devices must share the same value. When the set of devices is\n// empty, any device that has the attribute can be added. After that,\n// only matching devices can be added.\n//\n// We don't need to track *which* devices are part of the set, only\n// how many.\ntype matchAttributeConstraint struct {\n\tlogger        klog.Logger // Includes name and attribute name, so no need to repeat in log messages.\n\trequestNames  sets.Set[string]\n\tattributeName resourceapi.FullyQualifiedName\n\n\tattribute  *resourceapi.DeviceAttribute\n\tnumDevices int\n}\n\nfunc (m *matchAttributeConstraint) add(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID) bool {\n\tif m.requestNames.Len() > 0 && !m.matches(requestName, subRequestName) {\n\t\t// Device not affected by constraint.\n\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Constraint does not apply to request\", \"request\", requestName)\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tattribute := lookupAttribute(device, deviceID, m.attributeName)\n\tif attribute == nil {\n\t\t// Doesn't have the attribute.\n\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Constraint not satisfied, attribute not set\")\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tif m.numDevices == 0 {\n\t\t// The first device can always get picked.\n\t\tm.attribute = attribute\n\t\tm.numDevices = 1\n\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"First in set\")\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase attribute.StringValue != nil:\n\t\tif m.attribute.StringValue == nil || *attribute.StringValue != *m.attribute.StringValue {\n\t\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"String values different\")\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\tcase attribute.IntValue != nil:\n\t\tif m.attribute.IntValue == nil || *attribute.IntValue != *m.attribute.IntValue {\n\t\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Int values different\")\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\tcase attribute.BoolValue != nil:\n\t\tif m.attribute.BoolValue == nil || *attribute.BoolValue != *m.attribute.BoolValue {\n\t\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Bool values different\")\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\tcase attribute.VersionValue != nil:\n\t\t// semver 2.0.0 requires that version strings are in their\n\t\t// minimal form (in particular, no leading zeros). Therefore a\n\t\t// strict \"exact equal\" check can do a string comparison.\n\t\tif m.attribute.VersionValue == nil || *attribute.VersionValue != *m.attribute.VersionValue {\n\t\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Version values different\")\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Unknown value type, cannot match.\n\t\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Match attribute type unknown\")\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tm.numDevices++\n\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Constraint satisfied by device\", \"device\", deviceID, \"numDevices\", m.numDevices)\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (m *matchAttributeConstraint) remove(requestName, subRequestName string, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID) {\n\tif m.requestNames.Len() > 0 && !m.matches(requestName, subRequestName) {\n\t\t// Device not affected by constraint.\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tm.numDevices--\n\tm.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device removed from constraint set\", \"device\", deviceID, \"numDevices\", m.numDevices)\n}\n\nfunc (m *matchAttributeConstraint) matches(requestName, subRequestName string) bool {\n\tif subRequestName == \"\" {\n\t\treturn m.requestNames.Has(requestName)\n\t} else {\n\t\tfullSubRequestName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", requestName, subRequestName)\n\t\treturn m.requestNames.Has(requestName) || m.requestNames.Has(fullSubRequestName)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc lookupAttribute(device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID, attributeName resourceapi.FullyQualifiedName) *resourceapi.DeviceAttribute {\n\t// Fully-qualified match?\n\tif attr, ok := device.Attributes[resourceapi.QualifiedName(attributeName)]; ok {\n\t\treturn &attr\n\t}\n\tindex := strings.Index(string(attributeName), \"/\")\n\tif index < 0 {\n\t\t// Should not happen for a valid fully qualified name.\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif string(attributeName[0:index]) != deviceID.Driver.String() {\n\t\t// Not an attribute of the driver and not found above,\n\t\t// so it is not available.\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Domain matches the driver, so let's check just the ID.\n\tif attr, ok := device.Attributes[resourceapi.QualifiedName(attributeName[index+1:])]; ok {\n\t\treturn &attr\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// allocateOne iterates over all eligible devices (not in use, match selector,\n// satisfy constraints) for a specific required device. It returns true if\n// everything got allocated, an error if allocation needs to stop.\n//\n// allocateSubRequest is true when trying to allocate one particular subrequest.\n// This allows the logic for subrequests to call allocateOne with the same\n// device index without causing infinite recursion.\nfunc (alloc *allocator) allocateOne(r deviceIndices, allocateSubRequest bool) (bool, error) {\n\talloc.numAllocateOneInvocations.Add(1)\n\n\tif alloc.ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"filter operation aborted: %w\", context.Cause(alloc.ctx))\n\t}\n\n\tif r.claimIndex >= len(alloc.claimsToAllocate) {\n\t\t// Done! If we were doing scoring, we would compare the current allocation result\n\t\t// against the previous one, keep the best, and continue. Without scoring, we stop\n\t\t// and use the first solution.\n\t\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Allocation result found\")\n\t\treturn true, nil\n\t}\n\n\tclaim := alloc.claimsToAllocate[r.claimIndex]\n\tif r.requestIndex >= len(claim.Spec.Devices.Requests) {\n\t\t// Done with the claim, continue with the next one.\n\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex + 1}, false)\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded) {\n\t\t\t// We don't need to propagate this further because\n\t\t\t// this is not a fatal error. Retrying the claim under\n\t\t\t// different circumstances may succeed if it uses\n\t\t\t// subrequests and changing the allocation of some\n\t\t\t// prior claim enables allocating a subrequest here\n\t\t\t// which needs fewer devices.\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn success, err\n\t}\n\n\t// r.subRequestIndex is zero unless the for loop below is in the\n\t// recursion chain.\n\trequestKey := requestIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, subRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex}\n\trequestData := alloc.requestData[requestKey]\n\n\t// Subrequests are special: we only need to allocate one of them, then\n\t// we can move on to the next request. We enter this for loop when\n\t// hitting the first subrequest, but not if we are already working on a\n\t// specific subrequest.\n\tif !allocateSubRequest && requestData.parentRequest != nil {\n\t\t// Keep track of whether all attempts to do allocation with the\n\t\t// subrequests results in the allocation result limit exceeded.\n\t\t// If so, there is no need to make attempts with other devices\n\t\t// in the previous request (if any), except when\n\t\t// it is a firstAvailable request where some sub-requests\n\t\t// need less devices than others.\n\t\tallAllocationExceeded := true\n\t\tfor subRequestIndex := 0; ; subRequestIndex++ {\n\t\t\tnextSubRequestKey := requestKey\n\t\t\tnextSubRequestKey.subRequestIndex = subRequestIndex\n\t\t\tif _, ok := alloc.requestData[nextSubRequestKey]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t// Past the end of the subrequests without finding a solution -> give up.\n\t\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t\t// Return errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded if all\n\t\t\t\t// attempts for the subrequests failed to due to reaching\n\t\t\t\t// the max size limit. This would mean that there are no\n\t\t\t\t// solution that involves the previous request (if any).\n\t\t\t\tif allAllocationExceeded {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false, errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tr.subRequestIndex = subRequestIndex\n\t\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.allocateOne(r, true /* prevent infinite recusion */)\n\t\t\t// If we reached the allocation result limit, we can try\n\t\t\t// with the next subrequest if there is one. It might request\n\t\t\t// fewer devices, so it might succeed.\n\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If we get here, at least one of the subrequests failed for a\n\t\t\t// different reason than errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded.\n\t\t\tallAllocationExceeded = false\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If allocation with a subrequest succeeds, return without\n\t\t\t// attempting the remaining subrequests.\n\t\t\tif success {\n\t\t\t\t// Store the index of the selected subrequest\n\t\t\t\trequestData.selectedSubRequestIndex = subRequestIndex\n\t\t\t\talloc.requestData[requestKey] = requestData\n\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// This is unreachable, so no need to have a return statement here.\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up the current request that we are attempting to satisfy. This can\n\t// be either a request or a subrequest.\n\trequest := requestData.request\n\tdoAllDevices := request.allocationMode() == resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll\n\n\t// At least one device is required for 'All' allocation mode.\n\tif doAllDevices && len(requestData.allDevices) == 0 {\n\t\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Allocation for 'all' devices didn't succeed: no devices found\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"request\", requestData.request.name())\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// We already know how many devices per request are needed.\n\tif r.deviceIndex >= requestData.numDevices {\n\t\t// Done with request, continue with next one. We have completed the work for\n\t\t// the request or subrequest, so we can no longer be allocating devices for\n\t\t// a subrequest.\n\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex + 1}, false)\n\t\t// We want to propagate any errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded to the caller. If\n\t\t// that error is returned here, it means none of the requests/subrequests after this one\n\t\t// could be allocated while staying within the limit on the number of devices, so there\n\t\t// are no solution in the current request/subrequest that would work.\n\t\treturn success, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Before trying to allocate devices, check if allocating the devices\n\t// in the current request will put us over the threshold.\n\t// We can calculate this by adding the number of already allocated devices with the number\n\t// of devices in the current request, and then finally subtract the deviceIndex since we\n\t// don't want to double count any devices already allocated for the current request.\n\tnumDevicesAfterAlloc := len(alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices) + requestData.numDevices - r.deviceIndex\n\tif numDevicesAfterAlloc > resourceapi.AllocationResultsMaxSize {\n\t\t// Return a special error so we can identify this situation in the\n\t\t// callers and do more aggressive backtracking.\n\t\treturn false, errAllocationResultMaxSizeExceeded\n\t}\n\n\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Allocating one device\", \"currentClaim\", r.claimIndex,\n\t\t\"totalClaims\", len(alloc.claimsToAllocate), \"currentRequest\", r.requestIndex,\n\t\t\"currentSubRequest\", r.subRequestIndex, \"totalRequestsPerClaim\", len(claim.Spec.Devices.Requests),\n\t\t\"currentDevice\", r.deviceIndex, \"devicesPerRequest\", requestData.numDevices, \"allDevices\", doAllDevices, \"adminAccess\", request.adminAccess(), \"capacities\", request.capacities())\n\n\tif doAllDevices {\n\t\t// For \"all\" devices we already know which ones we need. We\n\t\t// just need to check whether we can use them.\n\t\tdeviceWithID := requestData.allDevices[r.deviceIndex]\n\t\tsuccess, deallocate, err := alloc.allocateDevice(r, deviceWithID, true)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !success {\n\t\t\t// The order in which we allocate \"all\" devices doesn't matter,\n\t\t\t// so we only try with the one which was up next. If we couldn't\n\t\t\t// get all of them, then there is no solution and we have to stop.\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tdone, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, deviceIndex: r.deviceIndex + 1}, allocateSubRequest)\n\t\tif err != nil || !done {\n\t\t\t// If we get an error or didn't complete, we need to backtrack. Depending\n\t\t\t// on the situation we might be able to retry, so we make sure we\n\t\t\t// deallocate.\n\t\t\tdeallocate()\n\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn done, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// We need to find suitable devices.\n\tfor _, pool := range alloc.pools {\n\t\t// If the pool is not valid, then fail now. It's okay when pools of one driver\n\t\t// are invalid if we allocate from some other pool, but it's not safe to\n\t\t// allocated from an invalid pool.\n\t\tif pool.IsInvalid {\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pool %s is invalid: %s\", pool.Pool, pool.InvalidReason)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, slice := range pool.Slices {\n\t\t\tfor deviceIndex := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n\t\t\t\tdeviceID := DeviceID{Driver: pool.Driver, Pool: pool.Pool, Device: slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex].Name}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Checking for \"in use\" is cheap and thus gets done first.\n\t\t\t\tif request.adminAccess() && alloc.allocatingDeviceForClaim(deviceID, r.claimIndex) {\n\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use in same claim\", \"device\", deviceID)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif !request.adminAccess() && alloc.deviceInUse(deviceID) {\n\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use\", \"device\", deviceID)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Next check selectors.\n\t\t\t\trequestKey := requestIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, subRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex}\n\t\t\t\tselectable, err := alloc.isSelectable(requestKey, requestData, slice, deviceIndex)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif !selectable {\n\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device not selectable\", \"device\", deviceID)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif alloc.features.ConsumableCapacity {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Next validate whether resource request over capacity\n\t\t\t\t\tdevice := slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex]\n\t\t\t\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.CmpRequestOverCapacity(requestData.request, slice, device)\n\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Skip comparing device capacity request\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"device\", deviceID, \"request\", requestData.request.name(), \"err\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif !success {\n\t\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device capacity not enough\", \"device\", deviceID)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Finally treat as allocated and move on to the next device.\n\t\t\t\tdevice := deviceWithID{\n\t\t\t\t\tid:     deviceID,\n\t\t\t\t\tDevice: &slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex],\n\t\t\t\t\tslice:  slice,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tallocated, deallocate, err := alloc.allocateDevice(r, device, false)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif !allocated {\n\t\t\t\t\t// In use or constraint violated...\n\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device not usable\", \"device\", deviceID)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdeviceKey := deviceIndices{\n\t\t\t\t\tclaimIndex:      r.claimIndex,\n\t\t\t\t\trequestIndex:    r.requestIndex,\n\t\t\t\t\tsubRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex,\n\t\t\t\t\tdeviceIndex:     r.deviceIndex + 1,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdone, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceKey, allocateSubRequest)\n\t\t\t\t// If we found a solution, we can stop.\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil && done {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn done, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Otherwise we didn't find a solution, and we need to deallocate\n\t\t\t\t// so the temporary allocation is correct for trying other devices.\n\t\t\t\tdeallocate()\n\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// If we hit an error, we return. This might be that we reached\n\t\t\t\t\t// the allocation size limit, and if so, it will be caught further\n\t\t\t\t\t// up the stack and other subrequests will be attempted if there\n\t\t\t\t\t// are any.\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If we get here without finding a solution, then there is none.\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\n// isSelectable checks whether a device satisfies the request and class selectors.\nfunc (alloc *allocator) isSelectable(r requestIndices, requestData requestData, slice *draapi.ResourceSlice, deviceIndex int) (bool, error) {\n\tdevice := &slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex]\n\tif !alloc.features.DeviceBindingAndStatus &&\n\t\tlen(device.BindingConditions) > 0 {\n\t\t// Devices with binding conditions are not supported, feature is off.\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tdeviceID := DeviceID{Driver: slice.Spec.Driver, Pool: slice.Spec.Pool.Name, Device: slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex].Name}\n\tmatchKey := matchKey{DeviceID: deviceID, requestIndices: r}\n\tif matches, ok := alloc.deviceMatchesRequest[matchKey]; ok {\n\t\t// No need to check again.\n\t\treturn matches, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif requestData.class != nil {\n\t\tmatch, err := alloc.selectorsMatch(r, device, deviceID, requestData.class, requestData.class.Spec.Selectors)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !match {\n\t\t\talloc.deviceMatchesRequest[matchKey] = false\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\trequest := requestData.request\n\tmatch, err := alloc.selectorsMatch(r, device, deviceID, nil, request.selectors())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\tif !match {\n\t\talloc.deviceMatchesRequest[matchKey] = false\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection, false) {\n\t\tmatches, err := nodeMatches(alloc.node, ptr.Deref(device.NodeName, \"\"), ptr.Deref(device.AllNodes, false), device.NodeSelector)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matches {\n\t\t\talloc.deviceMatchesRequest[matchKey] = false\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\talloc.deviceMatchesRequest[matchKey] = true\n\treturn true, nil\n\n}\n\n// CmpRequestOverCapacity checks if the given device has sufficient remaining capacity\n// to satisfy the resource request.\n// Return true if success.\nfunc (alloc *allocator) CmpRequestOverCapacity(request requestAccessor, slice *draapi.ResourceSlice, device draapi.Device) (bool, error) {\n\tdeviceID := DeviceID{Driver: slice.Spec.Driver, Pool: slice.Spec.Pool.Name, Device: device.Name}\n\tallocatingCapacity := alloc.allocatingCapacity[deviceID]\n\tallowMultipleAllocations := device.AllowMultipleAllocations\n\tcapacities := device.Capacity\n\tif allocatedCapacity, found := alloc.allocatedState.AggregatedCapacity[deviceID]; found {\n\t\treturn CmpRequestOverCapacity(allocatedCapacity, request.capacities(), allowMultipleAllocations, capacities, allocatingCapacity)\n\t}\n\treturn CmpRequestOverCapacity(NewConsumedCapacity(), request.capacities(), allowMultipleAllocations, capacities, allocatingCapacity)\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) selectorsMatch(r requestIndices, device *draapi.Device, deviceID DeviceID, class *resourceapi.DeviceClass, selectors []resourceapi.DeviceSelector) (bool, error) {\n\tfor i, selector := range selectors {\n\t\texpr := alloc.celCache.GetOrCompile(selector.CEL.Expression)\n\t\tif expr.Error != nil {\n\t\t\t// Could happen if some future apiserver accepted some\n\t\t\t// future expression and then got downgraded. Normally\n\t\t\t// the \"stored expression\" mechanism prevents that, but\n\t\t\t// this code here might be more than one release older\n\t\t\t// than the cluster it runs in.\n\t\t\tif class != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"class %s: selector #%d: CEL compile error: %w\", class.Name, i, expr.Error)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s: selector #%d: CEL compile error: %w\", klog.KObj(alloc.claimsToAllocate[r.claimIndex]), i, expr.Error)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If this conversion turns out to be expensive, the CEL package could be converted\n\t\t// to use unique strings.\n\t\tvar d resourceapi.Device\n\t\tif err := draapi.Convert_api_Device_To_v1_Device(device, &d, nil); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"convert Device: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tmatches, details, err := expr.DeviceMatches(alloc.ctx, cel.Device{Driver: deviceID.Driver.String(), AllowMultipleAllocations: d.AllowMultipleAllocations, Attributes: d.Attributes, Capacity: d.Capacity})\n\t\tif class != nil {\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"CEL result\", \"device\", deviceID, \"class\", klog.KObj(class), \"selector\", i, \"expression\", selector.CEL.Expression, \"matches\", matches, \"actualCost\", ptr.Deref(details.ActualCost(), 0), \"err\", err)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"CEL result\", \"device\", deviceID, \"claim\", klog.KObj(alloc.claimsToAllocate[r.claimIndex]), \"selector\", i, \"expression\", selector.CEL.Expression, \"actualCost\", ptr.Deref(details.ActualCost(), 0), \"matches\", matches, \"err\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// TODO (future): more detailed errors which reference class resp. claim.\n\t\t\tif class != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"class %s: selector #%d: CEL runtime error: %w\", class.Name, i, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s: selector #%d: CEL runtime error: %w\", klog.KObj(alloc.claimsToAllocate[r.claimIndex]), i, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matches {\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// All of them match.\n\treturn true, nil\n}\n\n// allocateDevice checks device availability and constraints for one\n// candidate. The device must be selectable.\n//\n// r identifies the request for which allocation is attempted.\n// The device is the candidate under consideration.\n// If \"must\" is true, then it is okay (but not required) to return an error which describes in more detail why allocation wasn't possible.\n// This is used when the caller wants exactly this device and will give up if it cannot allocate it.\n//\n// If that candidate works out okay, the shared state gets updated\n// as if that candidate had been allocated. If allocation cannot continue later\n// and must try something else, then the rollback function can be invoked to\n// restore the previous state.\n//\n// The rollback function is only provided in case of a successful allocation\n// (true and no error).\nfunc (alloc *allocator) allocateDevice(r deviceIndices, device deviceWithID, must bool) (bool, func(), error) {\n\tclaim := alloc.claimsToAllocate[r.claimIndex]\n\trequestKey := requestIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, subRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex}\n\trequestData := alloc.requestData[requestKey]\n\trequest := requestData.request\n\tallowMultipleAllocations := false\n\tif alloc.features.ConsumableCapacity {\n\t\tallowMultipleAllocations = device.AllowMultipleAllocations != nil && *device.AllowMultipleAllocations\n\t}\n\tif !allowMultipleAllocations && request.adminAccess() && alloc.allocatingDeviceForClaim(device.id, r.claimIndex) {\n\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use in same claim\", \"device\", device.id)\n\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t}\n\tif !request.adminAccess() && alloc.deviceInUse(device.id) {\n\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use\", \"device\", device.id)\n\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Devices that consume counters can not be allocated if the PartitionableDevices feature\n\t// is not enabled.\n\tif !alloc.features.PartitionableDevices && len(device.ConsumesCounters) > 0 {\n\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device consumes counters, but the partitionable devices feature is not enabled\", \"device\", device.id)\n\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Skip counter availability check for devices that allow multiple allocation and some capacity has already in-use.\n\tskipCounterCheck := allowMultipleAllocations && alloc.deviceCapacityInUse(device.id)\n\n\t// The API validation logic has checked the ConsumesCounters referred should exist inside SharedCounters.\n\tif !skipCounterCheck && len(device.ConsumesCounters) > 0 {\n\t\t// If a device consumes counters from a counter set, verify that\n\t\t// there is sufficient counters available.\n\t\tok, err := alloc.checkAvailableCounters(device)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Insufficient counters\", \"device\", device.id)\n\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar parentRequestName string\n\tvar baseRequestName string\n\tvar subRequestName string\n\tif requestData.parentRequest == nil {\n\t\tbaseRequestName = requestData.request.name()\n\t} else {\n\t\tparentRequestName = requestData.parentRequest.name()\n\t\tbaseRequestName = parentRequestName\n\t\tsubRequestName = requestData.request.name()\n\t}\n\n\t// Might be tainted, in which case the taint has to be tolerated.\n\t// The check is skipped if the feature is disabled.\n\tif alloc.features.DeviceTaints && !allTaintsTolerated(device.Device, request) {\n\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// It's available. Now check constraints.\n\tfor i, constraint := range alloc.constraints[r.claimIndex] {\n\t\tadded := constraint.add(baseRequestName, subRequestName, device.Device, device.id)\n\t\tif !added {\n\t\t\tif must {\n\t\t\t\t// It does not make sense to declare a claim where a constraint prevents getting\n\t\t\t\t// all devices. Treat this as an error.\n\t\t\t\treturn false, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: cannot add device %s because a claim constraint would not be satisfied\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), device.id)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Roll back for all previous constraints before we return.\n\t\t\tfor e := 0; e < i; e++ {\n\t\t\t\talloc.constraints[r.claimIndex][e].remove(baseRequestName, subRequestName, device.Device, device.id)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// All constraints satisfied. Mark as in use (unless we do admin access or allow multiple allocations)\n\t// and record the result.\n\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device allocated\", \"device\", device.id)\n\n\tif alloc.allocatingDevices[device.id] == nil {\n\t\talloc.allocatingDevices[device.id] = make(sets.Set[int])\n\t}\n\tif !allowMultipleAllocations {\n\t\talloc.allocatingDevices[device.id].Insert(r.claimIndex)\n\t}\n\n\tconsumedCapacity := make(map[resourceapi.QualifiedName]resource.Quantity, 0)\n\tvar shareID *types.UID\n\tif alloc.features.ConsumableCapacity {\n\t\t// Validate whether resource request over capacity\n\t\tsuccess, err := alloc.CmpRequestOverCapacity(requestData.request, device.slice, *device.Device)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Failed to compare device capacity request\",\n\t\t\t\t\"device\", device, \"request\", requestData.request.name(), \"err\", err)\n\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !success {\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device capacity not enough\", \"device\", device)\n\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif allowMultipleAllocations {\n\t\t\tconsumedCapacity = GetConsumedCapacityFromRequest(request.capacities(), device.Capacity)\n\t\t\tshareID = GenerateNewShareID()\n\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device capacity allocated\", \"device\", device.id,\n\t\t\t\t\"consumed capacity\", klog.Format(consumedCapacity))\n\t\t\talloc.allocatingCapacity.Insert(NewDeviceConsumedCapacity(device.id, consumedCapacity))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := internalDeviceResult{\n\t\trequest:       request.name(),\n\t\tparentRequest: parentRequestName,\n\t\tid:            device.id,\n\t\tDevice:        device.Device,\n\t\tslice:         device.slice,\n\t\tshareID:       shareID,\n\t}\n\tif request.adminAccess() {\n\t\tresult.adminAccess = ptr.To(request.adminAccess())\n\t}\n\tif len(consumedCapacity) > 0 {\n\t\tresult.consumedCapacity = consumedCapacity\n\t}\n\tpreviousNumResults := len(alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices)\n\talloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices = append(alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices, result)\n\n\treturn true, func() {\n\t\tfor _, constraint := range alloc.constraints[r.claimIndex] {\n\t\t\tconstraint.remove(baseRequestName, subRequestName, device.Device, device.id)\n\t\t}\n\t\talloc.allocatingDevices[device.id].Delete(r.claimIndex)\n\t\tif allowMultipleAllocations {\n\t\t\trequestedResource := alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices[previousNumResults].consumedCapacity\n\t\t\tif requestedResource != nil {\n\t\t\t\talloc.allocatingCapacity.Remove(NewDeviceConsumedCapacity(device.id, requestedResource))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\talloc.allocatingDevices[device.id].Delete(r.claimIndex)\n\t\t\tif alloc.features.PartitionableDevices && len(device.ConsumesCounters) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\talloc.deallocateCountersForDevice(device)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Truncate, but keep the underlying slice.\n\t\talloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices = alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices[:previousNumResults]\n\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device deallocated\", \"device\", device.id)\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc allTaintsTolerated(device *draapi.Device, request requestAccessor) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range device.Taints {\n\t\tif !taintTolerated(taint, request) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc taintTolerated(taint resourceapi.DeviceTaint, request requestAccessor) bool {\n\tfor _, toleration := range request.tolerations() {\n\t\tif resourceclaim.ToleratesTaint(toleration, taint) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// checkAvailableCounters checks if there are enough counters available to allocate\n// the specified device.\n//\n// Gets called only if the partitionable devices feature is enabled and the device\n// consumes counters.\nfunc (alloc *allocator) checkAvailableCounters(device deviceWithID) (bool, error) {\n\tslice := device.slice\n\tsliceName := slice.Name\n\n\t// Check first if the available counters for this slice have already been\n\t// calculated.\n\talloc.mutex.RLock()\n\tavailableCountersForSlice, found := alloc.availableCounters[sliceName]\n\talloc.mutex.RUnlock()\n\t// If not, we need to do it now. But we store the result so it doesn't need\n\t// to be calculated again.\n\t// Since this is computed without holding the lock on the mutex, other goroutines\n\t// might also do this work. But the input will be the same to all of them, so\n\t// the result will also always be the same.\n\tif !found {\n\t\tavailableCountersForSlice = make(counterSets, len(slice.Spec.SharedCounters))\n\t\tfor _, counterSet := range slice.Spec.SharedCounters {\n\t\t\tavailableCountersForCounterSet := make(map[string]resourceapi.Counter, len(counterSet.Counters))\n\t\t\tfor name, c := range counterSet.Counters {\n\t\t\t\tavailableCountersForCounterSet[name] = c\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tavailableCountersForSlice[counterSet.Name] = availableCountersForCounterSet\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Update the data structure to reflect counters already consumed by allocated devices. This\n\t\t// only includes devices where the allocation process has completed, so this will never\n\t\t// change during the allocation process.\n\t\tfor _, device := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n\t\t\tdeviceID := DeviceID{\n\t\t\t\tDriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n\t\t\t\tPool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n\t\t\t\tDevice: device.Name,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Devices that aren't allocated doesn't consume any counters, so we don't\n\t\t\t// need to consider them.\n\t\t\tif !alloc.allocatedState.AllocatedDevices.Has(deviceID) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, deviceCounterConsumption := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n\t\t\t\tavailableCountersForCounterSet := availableCountersForSlice[deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet]\n\t\t\t\tfor name, c := range deviceCounterConsumption.Counters {\n\t\t\t\t\texistingCounter, ok := availableCountersForCounterSet[name]\n\t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// the API validation logic has been added to make sure the counters referred should exist in counter sets.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// This can potentially result in negative available counters. That is fine,\n\t\t\t\t\t// we just treat it as no counters available.\n\t\t\t\t\texistingCounter.Value.Sub(c.Value)\n\t\t\t\t\tavailableCountersForCounterSet[name] = existingCounter\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Note that we don't include devices in the alloc.allocatingDevices here since\n\t\t\t// counters consumed by devices for the current claims are tracked in\n\t\t\t// alloc.consumedCounters\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Set the available counters on the allocator so we don't have to\n\t\t// compute this again.\n\t\talloc.mutex.Lock()\n\t\talloc.availableCounters[sliceName] = availableCountersForSlice\n\t\talloc.mutex.Unlock()\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the consumedCounters data structure with the counters consumed\n\t// by the current device.\n\tconsumedCountersForSlice, found := alloc.consumedCounters[sliceName]\n\t// If no devices in the allocating state have consumed any counters from the current\n\t// slice, initialize the data structure.\n\tif !found {\n\t\tconsumedCountersForSlice = make(counterSets)\n\t\talloc.consumedCounters[sliceName] = consumedCountersForSlice\n\t}\n\tfor _, deviceCounterConsumption := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n\t\tconsumedCountersForCounterSet, found := consumedCountersForSlice[deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet]\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tconsumedCountersForCounterSet = make(map[string]resourceapi.Counter)\n\t\t\tconsumedCountersForSlice[deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet] = consumedCountersForCounterSet\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor name, c := range deviceCounterConsumption.Counters {\n\t\t\tconsumedCounters, found := consumedCountersForCounterSet[name]\n\t\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\t\tconsumedCountersForCounterSet[name] = c\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconsumedCounters.Value.Add(c.Value)\n\t\t\tconsumedCountersForCounterSet[name] = consumedCounters\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that we didn't exceed the availability of any counters by allocating\n\t// the current device. If we did, the current set of devices doesn't work, so we\n\t// update the consumed counters to no longer reflect the current device.\n\tfor availableCounterSetName, availableCounters := range availableCountersForSlice {\n\t\tconsumedCounters := consumedCountersForSlice[availableCounterSetName]\n\t\tfor availableCounterName, availableCounter := range availableCounters {\n\t\t\tconsumedCounter := consumedCounters[availableCounterName]\n\t\t\tif availableCounter.Value.Cmp(consumedCounter.Value) < 0 {\n\t\t\t\talloc.deallocateCountersForDevice(device)\n\t\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true, nil\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) deviceInUse(deviceID DeviceID) bool {\n\treturn alloc.allocatedState.AllocatedDevices.Has(deviceID) || alloc.allocatingDeviceForAnyClaim(deviceID)\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) deviceCapacityInUse(deviceID DeviceID) bool {\n\t_, found := alloc.allocatedState.AggregatedCapacity[deviceID]\n\treturn found || alloc.allocatingCapacityForAnyClaim(deviceID)\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) allocatingDeviceForAnyClaim(deviceID DeviceID) bool {\n\treturn alloc.allocatingDevices[deviceID].Len() > 0\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) allocatingDeviceForClaim(deviceID DeviceID, claimIndex int) bool {\n\treturn alloc.allocatingDevices[deviceID].Has(claimIndex)\n}\n\nfunc (alloc *allocator) allocatingCapacityForAnyClaim(deviceID DeviceID) bool {\n\t_, found := alloc.allocatingCapacity[deviceID]\n\treturn found\n}\n\n// deallocateCountersForDevice subtracts the consumed counters of the provided\n// device from the consumedCounters data structure.\nfunc (alloc *allocator) deallocateCountersForDevice(device deviceWithID) {\n\tslice := device.slice\n\tsliceName := slice.Name\n\n\tconsumedCountersForSlice := alloc.consumedCounters[sliceName]\n\tfor _, deviceCounterConsumption := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n\t\tcounterSetName := deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet\n\t\tconsumedCounterSet := consumedCountersForSlice[counterSetName]\n\t\tfor name, c := range deviceCounterConsumption.Counters {\n\t\t\tconsumedCounter := consumedCounterSet[name]\n\t\t\tconsumedCounter.Value.Sub(c.Value)\n\t\t\tconsumedCounterSet[name] = consumedCounter\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// createNodeSelector constructs a node selector for the one term. v1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tValues:   []string{targetNodeName},\n\t\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t\t}, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif nodeSelector != nil {\n\t\t\tswitch len(nodeSelector.NodeSelectorTerms) len(nodeSelector.NodeSelectorTerms))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(ns.NodeSelectorTerms[0].MatchFields) > 0 || len(ns.NodeSelectorTerms[0].MatchExpressions) > 0 {\n\t\t// We have a valid node ([#100084](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/100084), [@mborsz](https://github.com/mborsz)) [SIG API Machinery, Instrumentation and Scalability]\n--\n/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1930-    \nCHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1931-- Kubeadm: improve the validation of serviceSubnet and podSubnet.\n/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1932-  ServiceSubnet has to be limited in size, due to implementation details, and the mask can not allocate more than 20 bits.\n/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1933-  PodSubnet validates against the corresponding cluster \"--node-cidr-mask-size\" of the kube-controller-manager, it fail if the values are not compatible.\n/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1934-  kubeadm no longer sets the node-mask automatically on IPv6 deployments, you must check that your IPv6 service subnet mask is compatible with the default node mask /64 or set it accordingly.\nCHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md:1935:  Previously, for IPv6, if the podSubnet had a mask lower than /112, kubeadm calculated a node-mask to be multiple of eight and splitting the available bits to maximise the number used for nodes. ([#95723](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95723), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]\nCHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1936-- The deprecated flag --experimental-kustomize is now removed from kubeadm commands. The insecure address flags `--address` and `--insecure-bind-address` have no effect in kube-apiserver and will be removed in v1.24. The insecure port flags `--port` and `--insecure-port` may only be set to 0 and will be removed in v1.24. ([#95856](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95856), [@knight42](https://github.com/knight42), [SIG API Machinery, Node, Testing])\n/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1939-- Add dual-stack Services (alpha). Doing\n// this in the apiserver avoids having to update clients which might be unaware\n// of the status feature.\nfunc dropDeallocatedStatusDevices(newClaim, oldClaim *resource.ResourceClaim) {\n\tif draConsumableCapacityFeatureInUse(claim *resource.ResourceClaim) bool {\n\tif claim == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tif resourceclaimspec.DRAConsumableCapacityFeatureInUse(&claim.Spec) Its single valid value is \"true\".\n    36→\t// This is used only inside the scheduler.\n    37→\tExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation = \"resource.kubernetes.io/extended-resource-claim\"\n    38→\t// Resource device class prefix is for generating implicit extended resource\n    39→\t// name for a device class when its ExtendedResourceName field is not\n    40→\t// specified. The generated name is this prefix + the device class name.\n    41→\t// The generated name may not be a valid extended resource name for use\n    42→\t// in pod.Spec.Resources.Requests, in that case, a valid name has to be specified\n    43→\t// explicitly in device class.\n    44→\tResourceDeviceClassPrefix string = \"deviceclass.resource.kubernetes.io/\"\n    45→)\n    46→\n    47→// +genclient\n    48→// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n    49→// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n    50→// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n    51→\n    52→// ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources,\n    53→// managed by a common driver. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many\n    54→// ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.\n    55→//\n    56→// At the moment, the only supported resources are devices with attributes and capacities.\n    57→// Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name.\n    58→// The ResourceSlice in which a device gets published may change over time. The unique identifier\n    59→// for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.\n    60→//\n    61→// Whenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number\n    62→// and updates all ResourceSlices with that new number and new resource definitions. A consumer\n    63→// must only use ResourceSlices with the highest generation number and ignore all others.\n    64→//\n    65→// When allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when\n    66→// looking for the best solution among several different alternatives, a\n    67→// consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in\n    68→// each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and\n    69→// if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.\n    70→//\n    71→// For resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead,\n    72→// the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.\n    73→//\n    74→// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n    75→// feature gate.\n    76→type ResourceSlice struct {\n    77→\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n    78→\t// Standard object metadata\n    79→\t// +optional\n    80→\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n    81→\n    82→\t// Contains the information published by the driver.\n    83→\t//\n    84→\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n    85→\tSpec ResourceSliceSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n    86→}\n    87→\n    88→const (\n    89→\t// ResourceSliceSelectorNodeName can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    90→\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.NodeName].\n    91→\tResourceSliceSelectorNodeName = \"spec.nodeName\"\n    92→\t// ResourceSliceSelectorDriver can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    93→\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.Driver].\n    94→\tResourceSliceSelectorDriver = \"spec.driver\"\n    95→)\n    96→\n    97→// ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice.\n    98→type ResourceSliceSpec struct {\n    99→\t// Driver identifies the DRA driver providing the capacity information.\n   100→\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   101→\t// objects with a certain driver name.\n   102→\t//\n   103→\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n   104→\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n   105→\t// This field is immutable.\n   106→\t//\n   107→\t// +required\n   108→\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n   109→\n   110→\t// Pool describes the pool that this ResourceSlice belongs to.\n   111→\t//\n   112→\t// +required\n   113→\tPool ResourcePool `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=pool\"`\n   114→\n   115→\t// NodeName identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool.\n   116→\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   117→\t// objects belonging to a certain node.\n   118→\t//\n   119→\t// This field can be used to limit access from nodes to ResourceSlices with\n   120→\t// the same node name. It also indicates to autoscalers that adding\n   121→\t// new nodes of the same type as some old node might also make new\n   122→\t// resources available.\n   123→\t//\n   124→\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   125→\t// This field is immutable.\n   126→\t//\n   127→\t// +optional\n   128→\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   129→\tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n   130→\n   131→\t// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,\n   132→\t// when that pool is not limited to a single node.\n   133→\t//\n   134→\t// Must use exactly one term.\n   135→\t//\n   136→\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   137→\t//\n   138→\t// +optional\n   139→\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   140→\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n   141→\n   142→\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the resources in the pool.\n   143→\t//\n   144→\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   145→\t//\n   146→\t// +optional\n   147→\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   148→\tAllNodes *bool `json:\"allNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allNodes\"`\n   149→\n   150→\t// Devices lists some or all of the devices in this pool.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ([#132467](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132467), [@sdowell](https://github.com/sdowell))\n- Fixed an issue where Windows kube-proxy’s `ModifyLoadBalancer` API updates did not match the HNS state in version 15.4. Support for `ModifyLoadBalancer` policy began with Kubernetes 1.31+. ([#131506](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131506), [@princepereira](https://github.com/princepereira))\n- Fixed an issue where `insufficientResources` was logged as a pointer during pod preemption, making logs more readable. ([#132183](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132183), [@chrisy-x](https://github.com/chrisy-x)) [SIG Node]\n- Fixed an issue where the kubelet token cache returned stale tokens when service accounts were recreated with the same name. The cache is now UID-aware. Additionally, the new `TokenRequestServiceAccountUIDValidation` feature gate (Beta, enabled by default) ensures the `TokenRequest` UID matches the service account UID when set. ([#132803](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132803), [@aramase](https://github.com/aramase)) [SIG API Machinery, Auth, Node and Testing]\n- Fixed bug that prevented the alpha feature `PodTopologyLabelAdmission` from working due to checking for the incorrect label key when copying topology labels. This bug delayed the graduation of the feature to beta by an additional release to allow time for meaningful feedback. ([#132462](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132462), [@munnerz](https://github.com/munnerz))\n- Fixed incorrect behavior for AllocationMode: All in ResourceClaim when used in subrequests. ([#131660](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131660), [@mortent](https://github.com/mortent)) [SIG Node]\n- Fixed misleading response codes in admission control metrics. ([#132165](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132165), [@gavinkflam](https://github.com/gavinkflam)) [SIG API Machinery, Architecture and Instrumentation]\n- Fixed runtime cost estimation for `x-int-or-string` custom resource schemas with maximum lengths. ([#132837](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132837), [@JoelSpeed](https://github.com/JoelSpeed))\n- Fixed the `allocatedResourceStatuses` field name mismatch in PVC status validation. ([#131213](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131213), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory))\n- Fixed the `observedGeneration` field in pod resize conditions to accurately reflect the associated pod generation when both `InPlacePodVerticalScaling` and `PodObservedGenerationTracking` feature gates are enabled. ([#131157](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131157), [@natasha41575](https://github.com/natasha41575))\n- Fixed the bug when swap related metrics were not available in `/metrics/resource` endpoint. ([#132065](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/132065), [@yuanwang04](https://github.com/yuanwang04)) [SIG Node and Testing]\n--\n- Fixed a bug that caused duplicate validation when updating a ReplicaSet. ([#131873](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131873), [@gavinkflam](https://github.com/gavinkflam)) [SIG Apps]\n- Fixed a panic issue related to kubectl revision history kubernetes/kubectl#1724 ([#130503](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/130503), [@tahacodes](https://github.com/tahacodes)) [SIG CLI]\n- Fixed a possible deadlock in the watch client that could happen if the watch was not stopped. ([#131266](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131266), [@karlkfi](https://github.com/karlkfi)) [SIG API Machinery]\n- Fixed an incorrect reference to `JoinConfigurationKind` in the error message when no ResetConfiguration is found during `kubeadm reset` with the `--config` flag. ModifyLoadBalancer policy is supported from Kubernetes 1.31+. ([#131506](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131506), [@princepereira](https://github.com/princepereira)) [SIG Windows]\n- HPA controller will no longer emit a 'FailedRescale' event if a scale operation initially fails due to a conflict but succeeds after a retry; a 'SuccessfulRescale' event will be emitted instead. A 'FailedRescale' event is still emitted if retries are exhausted. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DMz4driMe4pCPeVL2jfp7S","type":"tool_result","content":"- kube-scheduler removed `AzureDiskLimits` ,`CinderLimits` `EBSLimits` and `GCEPDLimits` plugin. Given the corresponding CSI driver reports how many volumes a node can handle in NodeGetInfoResponse, the kubelet stores this limit in CSINode and the scheduler then knows the limit of the driver on the node. Removed plugins AzureDiskLimits, CinderLimits, EBSLimits and GCEPDLimits if you explicitly enabled them in the scheduler config. ([#124003](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/124003), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory)) [SIG Scheduling, Storage and Testing]\n- kubelet: the `--image-credential-provider-config` file was loaded with strict deserialization, which failed if the config file contained duplicate or unknown fields. This protected against accidentally running with malformed config files, unindented files, or typos in field names, and it prevented unexpected behavior. ([#128062](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128062), [@aramase](https://github.com/aramase)) [SIG Auth and Node]\n- NodeRestriction admission now validates the audience value that kubelet is requesting a service account token for is part of the pod spec volume. This change is introduced with a new kube-apiserver featuregate `ServiceAccountNodeAudienceRestriction` that's enabled by default. ([#128077](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128077), [@aramase](https://github.com/aramase)) [SIG Auth, Storage and Testing]\n- Promoted `CustomResourceFieldSelectors` to stable; the feature was enabled by default. The `--feature-gates=CustomResourceFieldSelectors=true` flag was no longer needed on kube-apiserver binaries and would be removed in a future release. ([#127673](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/127673), [@jpbetz](https://github.com/jpbetz)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]\n- Promoted feature gate `StatefulSetAutoDeletePVC` from beta to stable. ([#128247](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128247), [@mattcary](https://github.com/mattcary)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth and Testing]\n- Removed all support for _classic_ dynamic resource allocation (DRA). The `DRAControlPlaneController` feature gate, formerly alpha, is no longer available. Kubernetes now only uses the _structured parameters_ model (also alpha) for allocating dynamic resources to Pods.\n\n  if and only if classic DRA was enabled in a cluster, remove all workloads (pods, app deployments, etc. ) which depend on classic DRA and make sure that all PodSchedulingContext resources are gone before upgrading. PodSchedulingContext resources cannot be removed through the apiserver after an upgrade and workloads would not work properly. ([#128003](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128003), [@pohly](https://github.com/pohly)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth, Etcd, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\n- Removed generally available feature gate `HPAContainerMetrics` ([#126862](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/126862), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Autoscaling]\n- Removed restrictions on subresource flag in kubectl commands ([#128296](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128296), [@AnishShah](https://github.com/AnishShah)) [SIG CLI]\n- Revised the kubelet API Authorization with new subresources, that allow finer-grained authorization checks and access control for kubelet endpoints.\n--\n- Added enforcement of an upper cost bound for DRA evaluations of CEL. The API server and scheduler now enforce an upper bound on the cost and runtime steps required for evaluating a CEL expression. ([#128101](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128101), [@pohly](https://github.com/pohly)) [SIG API Machinery and Node]\n- Annotation `batch.kubernetes.io/cronjob-scheduled-timestamp` added to Job objects scheduled from CronJobs is promoted to stable ([#128336](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128336), [@soltysh](https://github.com/soltysh)) [SIG Apps]\n- Apply fsGroup policy for ReadWriteOncePod volumes ([#128244](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128244), [@gnufied](https://github.com/gnufied)) [SIG Storage and Testing]\n- Graduate Job's ManagedBy field to Beta ([#127402](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/127402), [@mimowo](https://github.com/mimowo)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Testing]\n- Kube-apiserver: Promoted the `StructuredAuthorizationConfiguration` feature gate to GA. The `--authorization-config` flag now accepts `AuthorizationConfiguration` in version `apiserver.config.k8s.io/v1` (with no changes from `apiserver.config.k8s.io/v1beta1`). ([#128172](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128172), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG API Machinery, Auth and Testing]\n- Removed all support for _classic_ dynamic resource allocation (DRA). The `DRAControlPlaneController` feature gate, formerly alpha, is no longer available. Kubernetes now only uses the _structured parameters_ model (also alpha) for allocating dynamic resources to Pods.\n  \n  if and only if classic DRA was enabled in a cluster, remove all workloads (pods, app deployments, etc. ) which depend on classic DRA and make sure that all PodSchedulingContext resources are gone before upgrading. PodSchedulingContext resources cannot be removed through the apiserver after an upgrade and workloads would not work properly. ([#128003](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/128003), [@pohly](https://github.com/pohly)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth, Etcd, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\n- Revised the Kubelet API Authorization with new subresources, that allow finer-grained authorization checks and access control for kubelet endpoints.\n  Provided you enable the `KubeletFineGrainedAuthz` feature gate, you can access kubelet's `/healthz` endpoint by granting the caller `nodes/helathz` permission in RBAC.\n  Similarly you can also access  kubelet's `/pods` endpoint to fetch a list of Pods bound to that node by granting the caller `nodes/pods` permission in RBAC.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_0189Tixku69RtQQ4HsdbavVn","session_id":"5a9abf1c-b6a1-4e14-8463-451c4e8c5af5","uuid":"c008a233-6052-49e8-96a4-bc4aa26a9154"}

They ignore\n   905→\t// all ordinary claims to the device with respect to access modes and\n   906→\t// any resource allocations.\n   907→\t//\n   908→\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n   909→\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n   910→\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n   911→\t//\n   912→\t// +optional\n   913→\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n   914→\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n   915→\n   916→\t// If specified, the request's tolerations.\n   917→\t//\n   918→\t// Tolerations for NoSchedule are required to allocate a\n   919→\t// device which has a taint with that effect. The same applies\n   920→\t// to NoExecute.\n   921→\t//\n   922→\t// In addition, should any of the allocated devices get tainted\n   923→\t// with NoExecute after allocation and that effect is not tolerated,\n   924→\t// then all pods consuming the ResourceClaim get deleted to evict\n   925→\t// them. The scheduler will not let new pods reserve the claim while\n   926→\t// it has these tainted devices. Once all pods are evicted, the\n   927→\t// claim will get deallocated.\n   928→\t//\n   929→\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n   930→\t//\n   931→\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n   932→\t// feature gate.\n   933→\t//\n   934→\t// +optional\n   935→\t// +listType=atomic\n   936→\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n   937→\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n   938→\n   939→\t// Capacity define resource requirements against each capacity.\n   940→\t//\n   941→\t// If this field is unset and the device supports multiple allocations,\n   942→\t// the default value will be applied to each capacity according to requestPolicy.\n   943→\t// For the capacity that has no requestPolicy, default is the full capacity value.\n   944→\t//\n   945→\t// Applies to each device allocation.\n   946→\t// If Count > 1,\n   947→\t// the request fails if there aren't enough devices that meet the requirements.\n   948→\t// If AllocationMode is set to All,\n   949→\t// the request fails if there are devices that otherwise match the request,\n   950→\t// and have this capacity, with a value >= the requested amount, but which cannot be allocated to this request.\n   951→\t//\n   952→\t// +optional\n   953→\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n   954→\tCapacity *CapacityRequirements `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=capacity\"`\n   955→}\n   956→\n   957→// DeviceSubRequest describes a request for device provided in the\n   958→// claim.spec.devices.requests[].firstAvailable array. Each\n   959→// is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can\n   960→// also ask for several identical devices.\n   961→//\n   962→// DeviceSubRequest is similar to ExactDeviceRequest, but doesn't expose the\n   963→// AdminAccess field as that one is only supported when requesting a\n   964→// specific device.\n   965→type DeviceSubRequest struct {\n   966→\t// Name can be used to reference this subrequest in the list of constraints\n   967→\t// or the list of configurations for the claim. References must use the\n   968→\t// format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n   969→\t//\n   970→\t// Must be a DNS label.\n   971→\t//\n   972→\t// +required\n   973→\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   974→\n   975→\t// DeviceClassName references a specific DeviceClass, which can define\n   976→\t// additional configuration and selectors to be inherited by this\n   977→\t// subrequest.\n   978→\t//\n   979→\t// A class is required. Which classes are available depends on the cluster.\n   980→\t//\n   981→\t// Administrators may use this to restrict which devices may get\n   982→\t// requested by only installing classes with selectors for permitted\n   983→\t// devices. If users are free to request anything without restrictions,\n   984→\t// then administrators can create an empty DeviceClass for users\n   985→\t// to reference.\n   986→\t//\n   987→\t// +required\n   988→\t// +k8s:required\n   989→\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n   990→\tDeviceClassName string `json:\"deviceClassName\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceClassName\"`\n   991→\n   992→\t// Selectors define criteria which must be satisfied by a specific\n   993→\t// device in order for that device to be considered for this\n   994→\t// subrequest. All selectors must be satisfied for a device to be\n   995→\t// considered.\n   996→\t//\n   997→\t// +optional\n   998→\t// +listType=atomic\n   999→\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1000→\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=selectors\"`\n  1001→\n  1002→\t// AllocationMode and its related fields define how devices are allocated\n  1003→\t// to satisfy this subrequest. Supported values are:\n  1004→\t//\n  1005→\t// - ExactCount: This request is for a specific number of devices.\n  1006→\t//   This is the default. The exact number is provided in the\n  1007→\t//   count field.\n  1008→\t//\n  1009→\t// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.\n  1010→\t//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,\n  1011→\t//   unless adminAccess is requested.\n  1012→\t//\n  1013→\t// If AllocationMode is not specified, the default mode is ExactCount. If\n  1014→\t// the mode is ExactCount and count is not specified, the default count is\n  1015→\t// one. Any other subrequests must specify this field.\n  1016→\t//\n  1017→\t// More modes may get added in the future. Clients must refuse to handle\n  1018→\t// requests with unknown modes.\n  1019→\t//\n  1020→\t// +optional\n  1021→\tAllocationMode DeviceAllocationMode `json:\"allocationMode,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=allocationMode\"`\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.\\n\\nAt the moment, the only supported resources are devices with attributes and capacities. Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name. The ResourceSlice in which a device gets published may change over time. The unique identifier for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.\\n\\nWhenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number and updates all ResourceSlices with that new number and new resource definitions. A consumer must only use ResourceSlices with the highest generation number and ignore all others.\\n\\nWhen allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when looking for the best solution among several different alternatives, a consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.\\n\\nFor resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead, the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.\\n\\nThis is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.\",\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types_swagger_doc_generated.go-\t\"metadata\": \"Standard object metadata\",\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types_swagger_doc_generated.go-\t\"spec\":     \"Contains the information published by the driver.\\n\\nChanging the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\",\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types_swagger_doc_generated.go-}\n--\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types_swagger_doc_generated.go-\t\"\":                       \"ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice.\",\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types_swagger_doc_generated.go-\t\"driver\":                 \"Driver identifies the DRA driver providing the capacity information.
